muxt generate --use-receiver-type=Server
muxt check

# An sse() method that is not defined on the receiver gets a synthesized
# signature: the send callback becomes func(any) error and the method returns
# nothing, matching the void SSE contract.
grep 'Stream\(ctx context.Context, lastEventID string, send func\(any\) error\)' template_routes.go

exec go test -race -count=1

-- template.gohtml --
{{- define "GET /events sse(Stream(ctx, lastEventID, send))" -}}{{- .Result -}}{{- end -}}
-- go.mod --
module server

go 1.24
-- server.go --
package server

import (
	"context"
	"embed"
	"html/template"
)

//go:embed *.gohtml
var templatesFS embed.FS

var templates = template.Must(template.ParseFS(templatesFS, "*"))

type Server struct{}

// Stream implements the synthesized interface method signature. The callback
// accepts any; the template renders it as .Result.
func (Server) Stream(ctx context.Context, lastEventID string, send func(any) error) {
	_ = send("hi-" + lastEventID)
}
-- server_test.go --
package server

import (
	"net/http"
	"net/http/httptest"
	"strings"
	"testing"
)

func TestStream(t *testing.T) {
	mux := http.NewServeMux()
	TemplateRoutes(mux, Server{})

	req := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, "/events", nil)
	req.Header.Set("Last-Event-Id", "7")
	rec := httptest.NewRecorder()
	mux.ServeHTTP(rec, req)

	if got := rec.Body.String(); !strings.Contains(got, "data: hi-7\n\n") {
		t.Fatalf("body = %q, want it to contain %q", got, "data: hi-7\n\n")
	}
}
